words.definitionEn: often used at the end of negative sentences and at the end of questions, to mean ‘any longer’
words.exampleEn: She doesn't live here any more.

words.definitionEn: a feeling of wanting and expecting a particular thing to happen; something that you wish for
words.exampleEn: Don't lose hope—we'll find her.

words.definitionEn: used instead of somebody in negative sentences and in questions after if/whether, and after verbs such as prevent, forbid, avoid, etc.
words.exampleEn: Is there anybody who can help me?

words.definitionEn: extremely large in size or amount; great in degree
words.exampleEn: The sums of money involved are potentially huge.

words.definitionEn: of or connected with people rather than animals, machines or gods
words.exampleEn: the human body/brain

words.definitionEn: a person rather than an animal or a machine
words.exampleEn: Dogs can hear much better than humans.

words.definitionEn: used when adding something to support an idea or argument
words.exampleEn: It's too expensive and anyway the colour doesn't suit you.

words.definitionEn: injured physically
words.exampleEn: None of the passengers were badly hurt.

words.definitionEn: to cause physical pain to somebody/yourself; to injure somebody/yourself
words.exampleEn: hurt something, He hurt his back playing squash.

words.definitionEn: to take willingly something that is offered; to say ‘yes’ to an offer, invitation, etc.
words.exampleEn: He asked me to marry him and I accepted.

words.definitionEn: used in negative sentences and in questions instead of somewhere
words.exampleEn: I can't see it anywhere.

words.definitionEn: perfect; most suitable
words.exampleEn: an ideal location/place

words.definitionEn: used in negative sentences and in questions instead of somewhere
words.exampleEn: I don't have anywhere to stay.

words.definitionEn: to recognize somebody/something and be able to say who or what they are
words.exampleEn: identify somebody/something, She was able to identify her attacker.

words.definitionEn: suffering from an illness or disease; not feeling well
words.exampleEn: Her father is seriously ill in St Luke's hospital.

words.definitionEn: the state of being physically or mentally ill
words.exampleEn: The virus can cause illness in humans.

words.definitionEn: a picture, photograph or statue that represents somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Most simple leaflets will include text and images.

words.definitionEn: without delay
words.exampleEn: She answered almost immediately.

words.definitionEn: that cannot exist or be done; not possible
words.exampleEn: almost/virtually/nearly impossible

words.definitionEn: a piece of software that you can download to a device such as a smartphone or tablet, for example to look up information or to play a game
words.exampleEn: You first need to install the app on your device.

words.definitionEn: contained as part of something
words.exampleEn: all of Europe (Britain included)

words.definitionEn: having something as part of a group or set
words.exampleEn: I've got three days' holiday including New Year's Day.

words.definitionEn: a rise in the amount, number or value of something
words.exampleEn: price/tax/wage increases

words.definitionEn: to become greater in amount, number, value, etc.; to make something greater in amount, number, value, etc.
words.exampleEn: Costs have increased significantly.

words.definitionEn: impossible or very difficult to believe
words.exampleEn: an incredible story

words.definitionEn: having its own government
words.exampleEn: an independent state/nation/country

words.definitionEn: considered separately rather than as part of a group
words.exampleEn: We interviewed each individual member of the community.

words.definitionEn: a person considered separately rather than as part of a group
words.exampleEn: The competition is open to both teams and individuals.

words.definitionEn: the production of goods from raw materials, especially in factories
words.exampleEn: the needs of British industry

words.definitionEn: relaxed and friendly; not following strict rules of how to behave or do something
words.exampleEn: an informal atmosphere

words.definitionEn: to start to be seen
words.exampleEn: Three days later a rash appeared.

words.definitionEn: harm done to a person’s or an animal’s body, for example in an accident
words.exampleEn: Two people sustained minor injuries.

words.definitionEn: the way that somebody/something looks on the outside; what somebody/something seems to be
words.exampleEn: the physical/outward/external appearance of something

words.definitionEn: any small creature with six legs and a body divided into three parts. Insects usually also have wings. Ants, bees and flies are all insects.
words.exampleEn: Most birds spend most of the day eating insects.

words.definitionEn: forming the inner part of something; not on the outside
words.exampleEn: the inside pages of a newspaper

words.definitionEn: on or to the inside
words.exampleEn: She shook it to make sure there was nothing inside.

words.definitionEn: the inner part, side or surface of something
words.exampleEn: The inside of the box was blue.

words.definitionEn: on or to the inner part of something/somebody; within something/somebody
words.exampleEn: Go inside the house.

words.definitionEn: in the place of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Lee was ill so I went instead.

words.definitionEn: detailed information on how to do or use something
words.exampleEn: Follow the instructions on the packet carefully.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to teach somebody a practical skill or sport
words.exampleEn: a fitness/driving/ski instructor

words.definitionEn: an object used for producing musical sounds, for example a piano or a drum
words.exampleEn: to play an instrument

words.definitionEn: good at learning, understanding and thinking in a logical way about things; showing this ability
words.exampleEn: a highly intelligent child

words.definitionEn: connected with or involving two or more countries
words.exampleEn: international trade/law

words.definitionEn: to make a formal request, usually in writing, for something such as a job, a loan, permission for something, a place at a university, etc.
words.exampleEn: You should apply in person/by letter.

words.definitionEn: the first part of a book or speech that gives a general idea of what is to follow
words.exampleEn: a book with an excellent introduction and notes

words.definitionEn: to produce or design something that has not existed before
words.exampleEn: Who invented the steam engine?

words.definitionEn: a thing or an idea that has been invented
words.exampleEn: Fax machines were a wonderful invention at the time.

words.definitionEn: a spoken or written request to somebody to do something or to go somewhere
words.exampleEn: to issue/extend an invitation

words.definitionEn: to ask somebody to come to a social event
words.exampleEn: invite somebody to something, Have you been invited to their party?

words.definitionEn: if a situation, an event or an activity involves something, that thing is an important or necessary part or result of it
words.exampleEn: involve something, Any investment involves an element of risk.

words.definitionEn: one thing on a list of things to buy, do, talk about, etc.
words.exampleEn: What's the next item on the agenda?

words.definitionEn: used when the animal or thing that does an action is also affected by it
words.exampleEn: The cat was washing itself.

words.definitionEn: a thick sweet substance made by boiling fruit with sugar, often sold in jars and spread on bread
words.exampleEn: strawberry jam

words.definitionEn: a type of music with strong rhythms, in which the players often improvise (= make up the music as they are playing), originally created by African American musicians at the beginning of the 20th century
words.exampleEn: traditional/modern jazz

words.definitionEn: objects such as rings and necklaces that people wear as decoration
words.exampleEn: silver/gold jewellery

words.definitionEn: something that you say or do to make people laugh, for example a funny story that you tell
words.exampleEn: I can't tell jokes.

words.definitionEn: to say something to make people laugh; to tell a funny story
words.exampleEn: She was laughing and joking with the children.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to collect and write news stories for newspapers, magazines, radio, television or online news sites
words.exampleEn: a freelance journalist

words.definitionEn: an act of jumping
words.exampleEn: to do a parachute jump

words.definitionEn: to move quickly off the ground or away from a surface by pushing yourself with your legs and feet
words.exampleEn: ‘Quick, jump!’ he shouted.

words.definitionEn: a child or young person
words.exampleEn: Do you have any kids?

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/something die
words.exampleEn: kill (somebody/something), Cancer kills thousands of people every year.

words.definitionEn: the male ruler of an independent state that has a royal family
words.exampleEn: the kings and queens of England

words.definitionEn: the joint between the top and bottom parts of the leg where it bends in the middle
words.exampleEn: a knee injury

words.definitionEn: a sharp blade (= metal edge) with a handle, used for cutting or as a weapon
words.exampleEn: knives and forks

words.definitionEn: to hit a door, etc. in order to attract attention
words.exampleEn: He knocked three times and waited.

words.definitionEn: the information, understanding and skills that you gain through education or experience
words.exampleEn: scientific/technical knowledge and skills

words.definitionEn: a laboratory
words.exampleEn: a computer/research lab

words.definitionEn: a word used to mean ‘woman’ that some people, especially older people, consider is more polite
words.exampleEn: There's a lady waiting to see you.

words.definitionEn: a large area of water that is surrounded by land
words.exampleEn: in a lake, We swam in the lake.

words.definitionEn: a device that uses electricity, oil or gas to produce light
words.exampleEn: a desk/bedside lamp

words.definitionEn: to come down through the air onto the ground or another surface
words.exampleEn: The plane landed safely.

words.definitionEn: a small computer that can work with a battery and be easily carried
words.exampleEn: I opened my laptop and started typing.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is designing buildings
words.exampleEn: The house was designed by architect Louis Kahn.

words.definitionEn: after anyone or anything else; at the end
words.exampleEn: He came last in the race.

words.definitionEn: the person or thing that comes or happens after all other similar people or things
words.exampleEn: Sorry I'm late—am I the last?

words.definitionEn: to continue for a particular period of time
words.exampleEn: Each game lasts about an hour.

words.definitionEn: coming after something else or at a time in the future
words.exampleEn: Rembrandt's later works

words.definitionEn: the act or sound of laughing
words.exampleEn: to roar/howl with laughter

words.definitionEn: a rule that deals with a particular crime, agreement, etc.
words.exampleEn: to pass a law (= officially make it part of the system of laws)

words.definitionEn: the art and study of designing buildings
words.exampleEn: to study architecture

words.definitionEn: a person who is trained and qualified to advise people about the law and to represent them in court, and to write legal documents
words.exampleEn: to hire a lawyer

words.definitionEn: unwilling to work or be active; doing as little as possible
words.exampleEn: He was not stupid, just lazy.

words.definitionEn: to go with or in front of a person or an animal to show the way or to make them go in the right direction
words.exampleEn: If you lead, I'll follow.

words.definitionEn: a person who leads a group of people, especially the head of a country, an organization, etc.
words.exampleEn: party/world leaders

words.definitionEn: the process of learning something
words.exampleEn: lifelong/adult learning

words.definitionEn: to the smallest degree
words.exampleEn: He always turns up just when you least expect him.

words.definitionEn: smallest in size, amount, degree, etc.
words.exampleEn: He's the best teacher, even though he has the least experience.

words.definitionEn: smallest in size, amount, degree, etc.
words.exampleEn: He's the best teacher, even though he has the least experience.

words.definitionEn: a talk that is given to a group of people to teach them about a particular subject, often as part of a university or college course
words.exampleEn: to go to/attend a lecture

words.definitionEn: to give a talk or a series of talks to a group of people on a subject, especially as a way of teaching in a university or college
words.exampleEn: He has taught and lectured at the University of Pretoria.

words.definitionEn: to have the skill, intelligence, opportunity, etc. needed to do something
words.exampleEn: You must be able to speak French for this job.

words.definitionEn: a yellow citrus fruit with juice that has a bitter, sharp taste. Slices of lemon and lemon juice are used in cooking and drinks.
words.exampleEn: Add a squeeze of lemon juice.

words.definitionEn: to give something to somebody or allow them to use something that belongs to you, which they have to return to you later
words.exampleEn: lend something, They will lend equipment free of charge.

words.definitionEn: to a smaller degree; not so much
words.exampleEn: less expensive/likely/intelligent

words.definitionEn: used with uncountable nouns to mean ‘a smaller amount of’
words.exampleEn: less something, less butter/time/importance

words.definitionEn: to speak angrily to somebody because you disagree with them
words.exampleEn: My brothers are always arguing.

words.definitionEn: used with uncountable nouns to mean ‘a smaller amount of’
words.exampleEn: less something, less butter/time/importance

words.definitionEn: the height of something in relation to the ground or to what it used to be
words.exampleEn: The cables are buried one metre below ground level.

words.definitionEn: a conversation or discussion in which two or more people disagree, often angrily
words.exampleEn: to win/lose an argument

words.definitionEn: the way in which a person or a group of people lives and works
words.exampleEn: Many people are trying to adopt a healthy lifestyle these days.

words.definitionEn: a machine that carries people or goods up and down to different levels in a building or a mine
words.exampleEn: It's on the sixth floor—let's take the lift.

words.definitionEn: to raise somebody/something or be raised to a higher position or level
words.exampleEn: lift somebody/something, I lifted the lid of the box and peered in.

words.definitionEn: to make something start to burn
words.exampleEn: She lit a candle.

words.definitionEn: having a good chance of happening or being something; probable or expected
words.exampleEn: the most likely explanation/outcome/scenario

words.definitionEn: a connection between two or more people or things
words.exampleEn: link between A and B, Police suspect there may be a link between the two murders.

words.definitionEn: to make a physical or electronic connection between one object, machine, place, etc. and another
words.exampleEn: link A to B, The video cameras are linked to a powerful computer.

words.definitionEn: a person who listens
words.exampleEn: a good listener (= somebody who you can rely on to listen with attention or sympathy)

words.definitionEn: to a small degree
words.exampleEn: She seemed a little afraid of going inside.

words.definitionEn: a large organized group of soldiers who are trained to fight on land
words.exampleEn: The two opposing armies faced each other across the battlefield.

words.definitionEn: a device that keeps a door, window, box, etc. shut, usually needing a key to open it
words.exampleEn: She turned the key in the lock.

words.definitionEn: to fasten something with a lock; to be fastened with a lock
words.exampleEn: Did you lock the door?

words.definitionEn: an act of looking at somebody/something
words.exampleEn: look at somebody/something, Here, have a look at this.

words.definitionEn: to plan or organize something in advance
words.exampleEn: arrange something, The party was arranged quickly.

words.definitionEn: a large vehicle for carrying heavy loads by road
words.exampleEn: a lorry driver

words.definitionEn: unable to find your way; not knowing where you are
words.exampleEn: We always get lost in London.

words.definitionEn: a plan or preparation that you make so that something can happen
words.exampleEn: Travel and accommodation arrangements have been finalized.

words.definitionEn: making a lot of noise
words.exampleEn: loud laughter

words.definitionEn: in a way that makes a lot of noise or can be easily heard
words.exampleEn: Do you have to play that music so loud?

words.definitionEn: in a way that makes a lot of noise
words.exampleEn: She screamed as loudly as she could.

words.definitionEn: beautiful; attractive
words.exampleEn: She looked particularly lovely that night.

words.definitionEn: not high or tall; not far above the ground
words.exampleEn: a low wall/building/table

words.definitionEn: in or into a low position, not far above the ground
words.exampleEn: to crouch/bend low

words.definitionEn: good things that happen to you by chance, not because of your own efforts or abilities
words.exampleEn: with (any) luck, With any luck, we'll be home before dark.

words.definitionEn: having good luck
words.exampleEn: lucky to do something, His friend was killed and he knows he is lucky to be alive.

words.definitionEn: the official system used for sending and delivering letters, packages, etc.
words.exampleEn: a mail service/train/van

words.definitionEn: to send something to somebody using the postal system
words.exampleEn: mail something (to somebody/something), Don't forget to mail that letter to your mother.

words.definitionEn: very large or important
words.exampleEn: a major road

words.definitionEn: being a man or boy
words.exampleEn: a male friend/colleague/partner

words.definitionEn: a male person, animal or plant
words.exampleEn: The male of the species has a white tail.

words.definitionEn: to control or be in charge of a business, a team, an organization, land, etc.
words.exampleEn: to manage a business/factory/bank/hotel/soccer team

words.definitionEn: a person who is in charge of running a business, a shop or a similar organization or part of one
words.exampleEn: a bank/hotel manager

words.definitionEn: behaviour that is considered to be polite in a particular society or culture
words.exampleEn: to have good/bad manners

words.definitionEn: an unpleasant event, especially in a vehicle, that happens unexpectedly and causes injury or damage
words.exampleEn: a car/road/traffic accident
